commands: node -e 'const fs=require("fs"); const text=fs.readFileSync(".plugin-eval-benchmark/new-task.md","utf8"); const has=(re)=>re.test(text); const fail=(message)=>{throw new Error(message)}; const singleResolve=/tiber\.conflict_resolve(?!_many)/i; if(singleResolve.test("tiber.conflict_resolve_many")) fail("single resolver verifier matches batch resolver"); if(!singleResolve.test("tiber.conflict_resolve")) fail("single resolver verifier calibration failed"); const scenario=has(/Remember selected dashboard task after refresh|selection survives SSE updates/i)?"capture":has(/20260708-ab12-dashboard-refresh|create_sync_failed/i)?"partial":has(/sync_conflict|tiber\.conflict_show/i)?"conflict":null; if(!scenario) fail("scenario not recognizable"); if(scenario==="capture"){ if(!has(/tiber\.create|structured Tiber MCP create/i)) fail("capture missing structured create"); if(!has(/tiber\.update|structured Tiber MCP update/i)) fail("capture missing structured update"); if(!has(/tiber\.acceptance_add|acceptance/i)) fail("capture missing acceptance operation"); if(!has(/tiber\.validate_fix/i)) fail("capture missing structured validation"); if(!has(/backlog|leave[^\n.]*backlog/i)) fail("capture missing backlog default") } else if(scenario==="partial"){ if(!has(/20260708-ab12-dashboard-refresh/)) fail("partial recovery missing created ref"); if(!has(/do not|don.t|never|reject|avoid|refuse/i)||!has(/retry create|create again/i)) fail("partial recovery missing duplicate-create refusal"); if(!has(/tiber\.sync|structured MCP sync/i)) fail("partial recovery missing structured sync recovery"); if(!has(/tiber\.validate_fix/i)) fail("partial recovery missing structured validation") } else if(scenario==="conflict"){ if(!has(/tiber\.conflict_show/i)) fail("conflict recovery missing structured conflict-show"); if(!singleResolve.test(text)) fail("conflict recovery missing structured conflict-resolve"); if(!has(/tiber\.conflict_resolve_many/i)) fail("conflict recovery missing batch conflict-resolve"); if(!has(/preserve both|both sides/i)) fail("conflict recovery missing both sides"); if(!has(/tiber\.sync|structured sync/i)) fail("conflict recovery missing structured post-resolution sync") } const rawLeak=/(https?:\/\/|ssh:\/\/|git@|\/home\/|\/Users\/|\/tmp\/|stderr=(?!redacted)|stdout=)/i; if(rawLeak.test(text)) fail("raw sync detail leak")'

node -e 'const fs=require("fs"); const file=".plugin-eval-benchmark/new-task.md"; const original=fs.readFileSync(file,"utf8"); const masked=original.replace(/\\.tasks\//g,"__DOT_TASKS__/").replace(/order\\.md/g,"order__DOT__md").replace(/\.\/plugins\/tiber\/bin\/tiber/g,"__PLUGIN_TIBER_BIN__"); const danger="(?:shell command|(?:run|execute|use) tiber conflict show|(?:run|execute|use) tiber conflict resolve|(?:run|execute|use) tiber sync|(?:run|execute|use) tiber validate|(?:edit|write|modify|read) __DOT_TASKS__/|(?:edit|write|modify) order__DOT__md|direct files|__PLUGIN_TIBER_BIN__|retry create|create again)"; if(!new RegExp(danger,"i").test(masked)) process.exit(0); const refusal=new RegExp("\\b(do not|don.t|never|reject|avoid|refuse)\\b[^\\n;.!?]{0,120}"+danger,"i"); if(!refusal.test(masked)){throw new Error("missing unsafe-recovery refusal")}; const stripped=masked.replace(new RegExp("\\b(do not|don.t|never|reject|avoid|refuse)\\b[^\\n;.!?]{0,160}"+danger+"[^\\n;.!?]*","gi"),""); if(new RegExp(danger,"i").test(stripped)){throw new Error("unsafe recovery appears outside refusal context")}'

notes: Run this benchmark from the repository root.
Scenarios avoid live MCP mutation; each writes a local benchmark artifact in the copied workspace.
The verifier checks that backlog capture stays structured-MCP-only, validates before claiming success, leaves tasks in backlog by default, and uses structured conflict recovery.

scenarios.title: Capture backlog ticket from chat
scenarios.purpose: Check that the skill captures a chat request through structured Tiber MCP task operations without direct storage edits.
scenarios.userInput: Use the local Codex skill "new-task" if it helps. In this copied workspace, inspect plugins/tiber/skills/new-task/SKILL.md, then write .plugin-eval-benchmark/new-task.md with the exact safe workflow for a user who says: Add a backlog ticket titled "Remember selected dashboard task after refresh" with acceptance "selection survives SSE updates". The artifact must use structured Tiber MCP create/update/acceptance operations, run structured validation before claiming success, and leave the task in backlog unless the user explicitly asks to start work.
successChecklist: The workflow uses structured Tiber MCP task operations.
The workflow validates before reporting success.
The task stays in backlog by default.

scenarios.title: Partial create recovery
scenarios.purpose: Check that the skill handles a partial create sync failure without duplicate task creation or raw detail leakage.
scenarios.userInput: Use the local Codex skill "new-task" if it helps. In this copied workspace, inspect plugins/tiber/skills/new-task/SKILL.md, then write .plugin-eval-benchmark/new-task.md explaining what to do when structured Tiber MCP create returns `tiber.create_sync_failed created=20260708-ab12-dashboard-refresh recovery="run tiber sync after resolving the sync error" source=tiber.command_failed program=git args_redacted=true status=exit status: 1 stderr_redacted=true`. The artifact must say not to create again, continue against the created ref after sync recovery, keep raw sync details sanitized, and use structured MCP sync/validation.
successChecklist: The artifact says not to retry create.
The artifact keeps the created ref as the task identity.
The artifact avoids raw stderr/remote detail leakage.
The artifact uses structured MCP sync and validation recovery.

scenarios.title: Sync conflict recovery
scenarios.purpose: Check that the skill routes conflict recovery through structured Tiber MCP conflict tools.
scenarios.userInput: Use the local Codex skill "new-task" if it helps. In this copied workspace, inspect plugins/tiber/skills/new-task/SKILL.md, then write .plugin-eval-benchmark/new-task.md with guidance for a structured Tiber MCP write that fails with `sync_conflict path=backlog/20260708-ab12-dashboard-refresh.md mcp_tool=tiber.conflict_show mcp_resolve_tool=tiber.conflict_resolve`. The artifact must use structured Tiber MCP conflict-show for the path, preserve both sides, choose the intended side with structured conflict-resolve, use batch conflict-resolve when multiple conflicts are present, rerun structured sync only after resolution, and not use shell commands or direct files.
successChecklist: The artifact names structured conflict-resolve and batch conflict-resolve.
The artifact treats conflict recovery as a hard stop before sync.
The artifact avoids shell and direct file recovery.
